question: Ethereum ETF Flows on February 6?
description:
This market will resolve to "Positive" if total Ethereum ETF flows on Friday, February 6, 2026 are greater than 0, and to "Negative" if they are less than 0.

If flows are exactly 0, the market will resolve to 50-50.

The resolution source is Farside Investors, specifically the ETF Flow tab available at https://farside.co.uk/eth/ in the "Total" column for the date specified in the title.

The total flows will be considered finalized for that day once flows for all ETF providers have been published. If data for any ETF provider remains unpublished by 12 PM ET, 2 days after the date specified in the title, the market will resolve based on all available data published up to that time.

If the resolution source becomes permanently unavailable or fails to publish any data by 12 PM ET, 2 days after the specified date, the market will resolve 50-50.

question: Microsoft (MSFT) Up or Down on February 5?
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the official NASDAQ closing price for Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) on Thursday, February 5, 2026 is higher than the official NASDAQ closing price for MSFT on the most recent prior trading day.

This market will resolve to "Down" if the official NASDAQ closing price for Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) on Thursday, February 5, 2026 is lower than the official NASDAQ closing price for MSFT on the most recent prior trading day.

E.g., ordinarily, a market on Monday would refer to the previous Friday for its most recent closing price, unless that Friday were a market holiday, in which case it would refer to Thursday, or the next most recent trading day.

If the two specified closing prices are exactly equal, this market will resolve 50-50. Note that all figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.

If MSFT does not trade at all during the regular session, the market will resolve 50-50.

If either of the relevant days are shortened (for example, due to a market holiday schedule), the official closing price published by NASDAQ for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If either of the relevant days have no official closing price (for example, due to a trading halt into the market close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is NASDAQ, specifically the exchange-certified Close values published at https://www.nasdaq.com/market-activity/quotes/historical.

question: Ethereum Up or Down - February 6, 8AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the ETH/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

question: Silver (SI) Up or Down on February 5?
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if, on Thursday, February 5, 2026, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Silver (SI) futures is higher than the previous trading day's official settlement price for the same Active Month contract.

This market will resolve to "Down" if, on Thursday, February 5, 2026, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Silver (SI) futures is lower than the previous trading day's official settlement price for the same Active Month contract.

For CME Silver (SI) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (March, May, July, September, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month becomes a non-active month effective on its First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates. If CME does not publish the settlement price for the listed trading day by 11:59 PM ET of the next trading day, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the Active Month changes between the previous trading day and Thursday, February 5, 2026, the market will continue using the same contract month used on the previous trading day for the purposes of determining whether the settlement price moved up or down.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Silver (SI) futures.

question: USD/JPY Up or Down on February 4?
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price ("C") for the USD/JPY candle for the final daily candle on the date specified (ET) in the title is higher than the close price for the final daily candle on the prior trading day once information is finalized for both specified dates. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of the week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market's resolution will be based solely on information from the "C" figure located at the top of the USD/JPY Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (https://www.investing.com/currencies/usd-jpy-chart).

description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) on February 5 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If the final session is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

This market will resolve to "Down" if, on Thursday, February 5, 2026, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Crude Oil (CL) futures is lower than the previous trading day's official settlement price for the same Active Month contract.

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example; if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates. If CME does not publish the settlement price for the listed trading day by 11:59 PM ET of the next trading day, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the Active Month changes between the previous trading day and Thursday, February 5, 2026, the market will continue using the same contract month used on the previous trading day for the purposes of determining whether the settlement price moved up or down.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Crude Oil (CL) futures.

question: Will Microsoft (MSFT) close above $420 on February 5?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) on February 5 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If the final session is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance. The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/MSFT/history, published under "Historical Prices."

question: USD/CAD Up or Down on February 4?
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price ("C") for the USD/CAD candle for the final daily candle on the date specified (ET) in the title is higher than the close price for the final daily candle on the prior trading day once information is finalized for both specified dates. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of the week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market's resolution will be based solely on information from the "C" figure located at the top of the USD/CAD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (https://www.investing.com/currencies/usd-cad-chart).

question: Google (GOOGL) Up or Down on February 5?
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the official NASDAQ closing price for Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) on Thursday, February 5, 2026 is higher than the official NASDAQ closing price for GOOGL on the most recent prior trading day.

This market will resolve to "Down" if the official NASDAQ closing price for Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) on Thursday, February 5, 2026 is lower than the official NASDAQ closing price for GOOGL on the most recent prior trading day.

E.g., ordinarily, a market on Monday would refer to the previous Friday for its most recent closing price, unless that Friday were a market holiday, in which case it would refer to Thursday, or the next most recent trading day.

If the two specified closing prices are exactly equal, this market will resolve 50-50. Note that all figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.

If GOOGL does not trade at all during the regular session, the market will resolve 50-50.

If either of the relevant days are shortened (for example, due to a market holiday schedule), the official closing price published by NASDAQ for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If either of the relevant days have no official closing price (for example, due to a trading halt into the market close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is NASDAQ, specifically the exchange-certified Close values published at https://www.nasdaq.com/market-activity/quotes/historical.

description:

This market will resolve to "Down" if the official FTSE 100 Index closing price for FTSE 100 (UKX) on Thursday, February 5, 2026 is lower than the official FTSE 100 Index closing price for UKX on the most recent prior trading day.

E.g., ordinarily, a market on Monday would refer to the previous Friday for its most recent closing price, unless that Friday were a market holiday, in which case it would refer to Thursday, or the next most recent trading day.

If UKX does not trade at all during the regular session, the market will resolve 50-50.

If either of the relevant days have no official closing price (for example, due to a trading halt into the market close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

This market will resolve to "Down" if the official Russell 2000 Index closing price for Russell 2000 (RUT) on Thursday, February 5, 2026 is lower than the official Russell 2000 Index closing price for RUT on the most recent prior trading day.

question: Total Kills Over/Under 56.5 in Game 1?
description:
In the upcoming match between REKONIX and MOUZ in the BLAST Slam Group Stage, scheduled for February 4 at 7:10AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 1.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 1 is 57 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

events.title: Dota 2: REKONIX vs MOUZ (BO1) - BLAST Slam Group Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 match between REKONIX and MOUZ in the BLAST Slam Group Stage, scheduled for February 4 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "REKONIX" if REKONIX win the match against MOUZ.

This market will resolve to "MOUZ" if MOUZ win the match against REKONIX.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.liquipedia.net/dota2/Main_Page.

question: Total Kills Over/Under 18.5 in Game 2?
description:
In the upcoming match between Weibo Gaming and Anyone's Legend in the LPL Group Ascend, scheduled for February 4 at 6:15AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 2.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 2 is 19 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

events.title: LoL: Weibo Gaming vs Anyone's Legend (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Weibo Gaming and Anyone's Legend in the LPL Group Ascend, scheduled for February 4 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Weibo Gaming" if Weibo Gaming win the match against Anyone's Legend.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend win the match against Weibo Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Main_Page.

In the upcoming match between Weibo Gaming and Anyone's Legend in the LPL Group Ascend, scheduled for February 4 at 6:15AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 3.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 3 is 35 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

question: GPT-5.3 released by February 21, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if OpenAI's GPT-5.3 model is made available to the general public by February 21, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

For this market to resolve to "Yes," GPT-5.3 must be launched and publicly accessible, including via open beta or open rolling waitlist signups. A closed beta or any form of private access will not suffice. The release must be clearly defined and publicly announced by OpenAI as being accessible to the general public.

GPT-5.3 refers to a product explicitly named GPT-5.3 (e.g., ChatGPT-5.3o,  would count), or one that is recognized as a successor to GPT-5.2, similar to the progression from GPT-5.1 to GPT-5.2. Products labeled as GPT-6 or similar will not count for this market's resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from OpenAI, with additional verification from a consensus of credible reporting.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - February 6, 9AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the BTC/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Will Forgent Power Solutions' market cap be less than $8.00B at market close on IPO day?
description:
This market will resolve based on Forgent Power Solutions’ market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

As of market creation, the IPO is scheduled to price on February 5, 2026 (ET). If no such IPO occurs by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before April 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on the specified company’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

question: Will AGI Inc.'s market cap be less than $2.75B at market close on IPO day?
description:
This market will resolve based on AGI Inc’s market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

As of market creation, the IPO is scheduled to price on February 11, 2026 (ET). If no such IPO occurs by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before April 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on the specified company’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. As of market creation, the IPO is scheduled to price on February 11, 2026 (ET). If no such IPO occurs by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before April 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on the specified company’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

question: Total Kills Over/Under 52.5 in Game 1?
description:
In the upcoming match between Xtreme Gaming and OG in the BLAST Slam Group Stage, scheduled for February 4 at 9:15AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 1.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 1 is 53 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

events.title: Dota 2: Xtreme Gaming vs OG (BO1) - BLAST Slam Group Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 match between Xtreme Gaming and OG in the BLAST Slam Group Stage, scheduled for February 4 at 8:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Xtreme Gaming" if Xtreme Gaming win the match against OG.

This market will resolve to "OG" if OG win the match against Xtreme Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.liquipedia.net/dota2/Main_Page.

question: Ethereum Up or Down - February 5, 9:45AM-10:00AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Ethereum price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the ETH/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/eth-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream ETH/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - February 6, 10AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the BTC/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - February 5, 10:00AM-10:15AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Bitcoin price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the BTC/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/btc-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream BTC/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: NBA Parlay: Thunder @ Spurs
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if all of the following occur during the Oklahoma City Thunder vs. San Antonio Spurs game originally scheduled for February 4, 2026, 9:30 PM ET:

-Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scores 30 or more points

-Victor Wembanyama records 3 or more blocks

-Chet Holmgren records 9 or more rebounds

-Stephon Castle records 7 or more assists

-Cason Wallace records 2 or more steals

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If this game is canceled or postponed past February 18, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

If a listed player does not participate for any reason in the Oklahoma City Thunder vs. San Antonio Spurs game originally scheduled for February 4, 2026, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source will be the official NBA box score as published on NBA.com.

question: Total Kills Over/Under 50.5 in Game 1?
description:
In the upcoming match between Heroic and Team Spirit in the BLAST Slam Group Stage, scheduled for February 4 at 10:30AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 1.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 1 is 51 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

events.title: Dota 2: Heroic vs Team Spirit (BO1) - BLAST Slam Group Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 match between Heroic and Team Spirit in the BLAST Slam Group Stage, scheduled for February 4 at 9:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Heroic" if Heroic win the match against Team Spirit.

This market will resolve to "Team Spirit" if Team Spirit win the match against Heroic.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.liquipedia.net/dota2/Main_Page.

question: Will ON Semiconductor say "Taiwan" during earnings call?
description:
This market will resolve based on the next earnings announcement of ON Semiconductor currently scheduled to take place on February 9, 2026 at 5PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone during this event. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A Q&A event will count toward the resolution of this market.

If this event is definitely cancelled, or otherwise is not aired by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be audio of the event.

question: Total Kills Over/Under 55.5 in Game 1?
description:
In the upcoming match between Heroic and Team Spirit in the BLAST Slam Group Stage, scheduled for February 4 at 10:30AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 1.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 1 is 56 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

events.title: Dota 2: Heroic vs Team Spirit (BO1) - BLAST Slam Group Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 match between Heroic and Team Spirit in the BLAST Slam Group Stage, scheduled for February 4 at 9:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Heroic" if Heroic win the match against Team Spirit.

This market will resolve to "Team Spirit" if Team Spirit win the match against Heroic.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.liquipedia.net/dota2/Main_Page.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - February 6, 11AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the BTC/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Big Game: Big Man TD?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if at least one big man touchdown is scored by either team at any point during Super Bowl LX, including overtime.

This market will resolve to “No” if no big man touchdowns are scored during Super Bowl LX, including overtime.

A “big man touchdown” is defined as a touchdown that is scored by a player officially listed as an offensive tackle, offensive guard, offensive lineman, or center. 

For the purpose of this market, only players listed by the Seattle Seahawks and New England Patriots as an offensive tackle, offensive guard, offensive lineman, or center on each team’s respective official roster will be considered.

Seattle Seahawks official roster source: https://www.seahawks.com/team/players-roster/

New England Patriots official roster source: https://www.patriots.com/team/players-roster/

For the purpose of this market, no other form of score, besides a touchdown, will resolve to “Yes”.

If Super Bowl LX is cancelled or postponed after February 22, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or if it is not determined within that timeframe whether a big man touchdown occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be the official NFL game book as published on NFL.com.

question: XRP Up or Down - February 5, 11:15AM-11:30AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the XRP price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the XRP/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/xrp-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream XRP/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.
